title: IacPlan
description: Normalized output of bin/iac-plan.sh — the tool-agnostic plan/diff summary for one IaC unit. Produced by detecting iac.tool, dispatching to bin/iac-plan/<tool>.sh, and normalizing whatever machine-readable output the tool offers (terraform `show -json`, pulumi `preview --json`, cdk diff parse) into a common shape. helm/kubernetes/kustomize/ansible are ADVISORY in v1.13.0: they emit a text diff, summary counts are 0, and `destructive` is reported via the `destructive_known:false` flag (treated as potentially-destructive by the apply gate). Read-only — iac-plan.sh never mutates infrastructure; it only previews. Soft-skips (status:'skipped') when the tool CLI or its backend/credentials are absent.

schema_version.description: IacPlan schema version. Bump in lockstep with the schema's $id when v2 ships.

status.description: planned: the tool produced a plan (structured or advisory). skipped: the CLI, backend, or credentials were absent, so no plan ran (soft-skip; exit 0). refused: a precondition failed (e.g. unknown tool, --unit escapes target).

tool.description: The detected iac.tool that produced this plan (long-form, schema-aligned). Empty string only when status is skipped/refused before a tool was resolved.

unit.description: Repo-relative path of the planned unit ('.' for the root unit). The whole target when --unit was not passed.

summary.description: Resource action counts parsed from the plan. For ADVISORY tools (helm/k8s/kustomize/ansible) and for skipped/refused plans these are all 0 — absence of a count is NOT a claim that the change is non-destructive (see destructive_known).

change.description: Resources to be updated in place.

destroy.description: Resources to be destroyed/replaced. Drives the destructive-apply gate.

destructive.description: True when this plan would destroy or replace at least one resource. For structured tools this is `summary.destroy > 0`. For ADVISORY tools it is conservatively TRUE (we cannot prove safety from a text diff), paired with destructive_known:false so the apply layer demands --confirm-destroy.

destructive_known.description: True when the destructive verdict was derived from a structured, machine-readable plan (terraform/opentofu/pulumi/aws-cdk). False for ADVISORY text-diff tools (helm/kubernetes/kustomize/ansible) where destruction cannot be reliably counted in v1.13.0 — the apply gate treats destructive_known:false as potentially-destructive.

raw_path.description: Absolute path to the captured raw tool output (terraform plan binary's JSON, pulumi preview JSON, or the advisory text diff) written under a private temp/working dir. Present when status is planned. NEVER contains credentials; MAY contain resource addresses and config values, so it is written outside the repo (never committed) and referenced by path only — the IacPlan JSON itself carries no raw plan contents.

message.description: Human-readable detail — the soft-skip reason (status:skipped), the refusal reason (status:refused), or a one-line summary line (status:planned).
